rulings.Case Title: CONSTANCE BOURGES VS. DANY SCHER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order On Defendant'S Demurrer
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, October 8, 2013, line 8 Plaintiff'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order On Defendant'S Demurrer. Plaintiff's Peremptory Challenge to Honorable Ronald Evans Quidachay is DENIED. Plaintiff's Motion for Reconsideration is DENIED; Plaintiff failed to provide sufficient notice per CCP 1005 and failed to sustain burden under 1008 - no new facts, law or circumstances not in existence at the time of the original hearing.

rulings.Case Title: UNION BANK, N.A. VS. FRANK MYERS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, October 8, 2013, line 2 CROSS DEFENDANT UNION BANK, N.A. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend as to causes of action for (1) dissolution of LLC; and (2) monetary, declaratory and injunctive relief. No opposition filed. Prevailing party prepare order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROSALIND NG VS. LEVY, SHIRLEY E., EXECUTOR OF THE ESTATE OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Expunge Notice Of Interest In Real Estate Holdings And Related Document
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, October 8, 2013, line 3 PLAINTIFF ROSALIND NG Notice Of Motion To Expunge Notice Of Interest In Real Estate Holdings And Related Document is DENIED. Moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that CCP 405.23 et seq. is applicable under the circumstances of this case. August 20, 2003 order is valid on its face and recorded pursuant to PC 7263. No evidence to the contrary is presented to the Court, i.e. that Trust does not have a 54% interest in the property pursuant to August 20, 2003 order as indicated in documents recorded on August 22, 2003 and October 25, 2011. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 2600 VAN NESS AVENUE LLC VS. LAURA BALLIS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, October 5, 2013, line 5 DEFENDANT LAURA BALLIS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Notice based on nuisance created by illegal activity sufficiently apprises the defendant of the grounds for eviction. Whether or not the alleged nuance substantially interfered with comfort, safety or enjoyment is an issue of fact that cannot be raised on demurer. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Vacate Default And Default Judgmnt
